question.text: For life insurance and investment-related insurance, what additional CDD must be conducted on beneficiaries?

answer.text: Obtain the full name including aliases of named beneficiaries, sufficient information for class beneficiaries, and determine if any beneficiary is a PEP
No additional CDD is required for beneficiaries of insurance policies
Only verify the beneficiary's identity at the time of policy purchase
Only screen beneficiaries against sanctions lists at the time of claim

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** For life insurance, the RE must: (i) obtain full name including aliases of named natural person beneficiaries; (ii) for class beneficiaries (e.g., spouse, children), obtain sufficient information to identify them at payout time; and (iii) determine if beneficiaries or their BOs are PEPs — at latest at the time of payout.
**The Trap:** Beneficiary CDD is ongoing — not just at policy inception. PEP determination must happen at the LATEST at payout time. Higher risks identified should be escalated to Senior Management before payout.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Insurance-specific CDD is unique to IFSCA-01's coverage since IFSCA regulates insurance in IFSC. Series XXIV doesn't cover insurance-specific provisions as it focuses on securities markets.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Insurance beneficiary CDD: named persons = get full name/aliases; class beneficiaries (spouse/children) = get enough info to identify at payout; PEP check = at latest at payout. If PEP, escalate to Senior Management.
